description: For nursing, pharmacy, clinical medicine, public health, and health administration roles. Emphasises clinical skills, compassion, and patient-centred care.

examples.title: Nursing Graduate Cover Letter for University Teaching Hospital
examples.body:
10 June 2026

Dear Nursing Director,

I am writing to apply for the Registered Nurse position at the University Teaching Hospital. I recently graduated from the University of Zambia with a Bachelor of Science in Nursing and Midwifery, and I am eager to begin my nursing career at an institution known for clinical excellence and professional development.

My nursing education has provided comprehensive clinical training across medical, surgical, paediatric, maternity, and community health settings. I have completed over 1,800 hours of clinical practice at Levy Mwanawasa Hospital, Ndola Teaching Hospital, and rural health centres. During these rotations, I developed skills in patient assessment, medication administration, wound care, intravenous therapy, and emergency response.

I am particularly passionate about maternal and child health. During my maternity rotation, I assisted in over 50 deliveries and provided antenatal and postnatal care to more than 100 mothers. I also participated in a community outreach programme that provided family planning education in rural communities, which reinforced my belief that nursing extends beyond the hospital walls.

I am a compassionate, resilient, and team-oriented nurse who understands that patients are not just cases, they are people with families, fears, and hopes. I am prepared to work night shifts, weekends, and holidays, because patient care does not follow business hours.

I would be honoured to serve under your nursing leadership and to contribute to the health and wellbeing of Zambian patients.

Yours faithfully,

Memory Nkhata

examples.title: Pharmacy Internship Application at Maina Soko Hospital
examples.body:
14 April 2026

Dear Chief Pharmacist,

I am applying for the Pharmacy Internship position at Maina Soko Military Hospital. I am a fourth-year Bachelor of Pharmacy student at the University of Zambia, and I am seeking practical experience in hospital pharmacy practice.

My academic training has covered pharmacology, pharmaceutics, clinical pharmacy, pharmacotherapy, and pharmacy management. I have completed coursework in drug interactions, adverse drug reaction monitoring, therapeutic drug monitoring, and antimicrobial stewardship. I am proficient in dispensing procedures, inventory management, and patient counselling.

During my third-year rotation at a community pharmacy, I dispensed over 500 prescriptions under supervision, counselled patients on medication adherence, and assisted in managing stock levels for a formulary of 300 items. I also identified three potential drug-drug interactions that the prescribing doctor had missed, which strengthened my commitment to the pharmacist's role as a medication safety guardian.

I am particularly interested in your hospital's focus on evidence-based medicine and multidisciplinary care. I believe that pharmacists should be integrated into clinical teams, contributing to treatment decisions rather than just dispensing medications. I am eager to learn from your experienced pharmacists and to develop the clinical judgement that distinguishes excellent pharmacists from competent ones.

I am available for a twelve-month internship commencing in August 2026.

Yours sincerely,

Kennedy Mutale

examples.title: Public Health Graduate Application for Ministry of Health
examples.body:
2 August 2026

Dear Permanent Secretary,

I am writing to apply for the Public Health Officer position at the Ministry of Health. I hold a Master of Public Health from the University of Zambia, with a specialisation in epidemiology and disease surveillance.

My graduate training has equipped me with advanced skills in epidemiological methods, biostatistics, health systems analysis, and programme evaluation. My thesis analysed the spatial distribution of malaria cases in Luapula Province using geographic information systems, identifying high-transmission clusters and correlating them with environmental factors like rainfall, temperature, and proximity to breeding sites. The findings were presented at the Zambia Public Health Association conference and are being considered for inclusion in the provincial malaria control strategy.

I also have practical field experience. During my Master's programme, I worked with the provincial health office to conduct a vaccination coverage survey in five districts. I trained data collectors, supervised fieldwork, cleaned and analysed the data, and wrote a technical report that informed resource allocation decisions.

I am deeply committed to improving health outcomes in Zambia through evidence-based public health practice. I believe that strong surveillance, timely data analysis, and effective communication between researchers and policymakers are the foundations of good public health.

I am eager to serve the Ministry and the Zambian people in this capacity.

Yours faithfully,

Eunice Chanda
